tools.description: Search indexed evidence with query_string syntax. Use for targeted lookups by indicator, user, IP, hash, or field value. Do not use for frequency counts (use opensearch_aggregate) or activity spikes (use opensearch_timeline); for one full document use opensearch_get_event. Example: opensearch_search(query='event.code:4688 AND process.name:*powershell*', case_id='rocba-drive-20260526-1417').

case_id.description: Case id. If empty, resolves to the active portal case (SIFT_CASE_DIR). Yields 'case-{id}-*'.
case_id.title: Case Id

compact.description: True excludes bloat fields and truncates values to 500 chars. Set False for full docs (prefer opensearch_get_event for one doc).
compact.title: Compact

index.description: Index pattern; every segment MUST start with 'case-'. Overrides case_id when set. Leave empty to derive from case_id/active case.
index.title: Index

limit.description: Max hits to return. Hard cap 200.
limit.title: Limit

offset.description: Pagination offset; capped at OpenSearch max_result_window (10000).
offset.title: Offset

query.description: OpenSearch query_string. Include file extensions ('svchost.exe' not 'svchost'); quote special chars (source.ip:"::1").
query.title: Query

meta.avoid_when: Avoid for frequency counts (use opensearch_aggregate), activity spikes (use opensearch_timeline), exact totals (use opensearch_count), or one full document (use opensearch_get_event). Avoid before evidence is ingested.
meta.defer_loading_rationale: Core analysis verb used in nearly every session; keep always-loaded.
meta.output_shape: SearchOut: total, total_capped (true ⇒ total is a gte lower bound — call opensearch_count for exact), returned, offset, compact, results[] of SearchHit{id, index, fields, truncated[]}, advisories[].
meta.response_shaping: compact=True (default) drops bloat fields and truncates values to 500 chars for context efficiency; raise limit/offset to page rather than widening the query. Use opensearch_get_event to expand a single hit.

usage_examples.description: Minimal: find a process by name
Scoped + time-bounded
Full document view, second page, oldest-first

meta.when_to_use: Targeted lookups by indicator, user, IP, hash, process, or field value across already-indexed case evidence. Supports query_string syntax, time_from/time_to bounds, sort, and offset pagination.

tools.name: opensearch_search

index.description: Concrete index the hit came from.
index.title: Index

truncated.description: Field names whose values were truncated to the size ceiling.
truncated.title: Truncated

total_capped.description: True when total is a lower bound (relation gte); call count for exact.
total_capped.title: Total Capped

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.title: Search Evidence

tools.description: Return an exact match count without documents. Use to verify index population or gauge magnitude before opensearch_search. Do not use when you need per-value counts; use opensearch_aggregate. Example: opensearch_count(query='event.code:4624').

case_id.description: Case id. If empty, resolves to the active portal case (SIFT_CASE_DIR). Yields 'case-{id}-*'.
case_id.title: Case Id

index.description: Index pattern; every segment MUST start with 'case-'. Overrides case_id when set. Leave empty to derive from case_id/active case.
index.title: Index

query.description: query_string filter; default '*' counts all docs in scope.
query.title: Query

meta.avoid_when: Avoid when you need per-value counts (use opensearch_aggregate) or the documents themselves (use opensearch_search).
meta.defer_loading_rationale: Low-frequency helper; opensearch_search already reports total. Tool-Search `defer_loading` candidate to trim the always-loaded set.
meta.output_shape: CountOut: count (exact document count for the query in scope).
meta.response_shaping: Cheapest possible probe — one integer, no docs. Use to decide whether a full search is worth paging.

usage_examples.description: Count all docs in scope
Count successful logons

meta.when_to_use: Get an EXACT match count with no documents — verify index population or gauge magnitude before a larger opensearch_search.

tools.name: opensearch_count

count.description: Exact document count for the query in scope.
count.title: Count

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.title: Count Documents

tools.description: List Security Analytics detection findings, or suggest a Hayabusa query when Sigma is unavailable or empty. Use to triage rule-based detections; severity filtering is applied behavior-compatibly. Example: opensearch_list_detections(severity='high').

detector_type.description: Detector type filter; empty returns all.
detector_type.title: Detector Type

severity.description: Severity filter; empty returns all severities.
severity.title: Severity

meta.avoid_when: Detection hits are leads, not conclusions — validate against source events and surrounding context before recording a finding.
meta.defer_loading_rationale: Depends on the optional Security Analytics plugin; lower-frequency. Tool-Search `defer_loading` candidate.
meta.output_shape: ListDetectionsOut: findings[] of Detection{id, timestamp, index, rules[] of {name, tags[]}, matched_docs}, total, returned, offset, suggestion (Hayabusa fallback when present).
meta.response_shaping: Paginate with limit/offset; the per-finding payload references matched docs by count, not by embedding them.

usage_examples.description: All detections, first page
High-severity detections only

meta.when_to_use: List Security Analytics (Sigma) detection findings for triage pivots, filtered by severity/detector_type; falls back to a Hayabusa query suggestion when the Sigma plugin is unavailable or empty.

tools.name: opensearch_list_detections

suggestion.description: Hayabusa fallback query when Sigma is unavailable or empty.
suggestion.title: Suggestion

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.title: Security Analytics Detections

tools.description: Correct a wrong host.id mapping in the active case by updating the case host dictionary and reindexing docs where host.name equals raw. host.name is never changed. Use when ingest applied a wrong canonical host id. Example: opensearch_fix_host_mapping(raw='wksn01', new_canonical='wksn01').

new_canonical.description: The correct canonical host.id to assign.
new_canonical.title: New Canonical

raw.description: The raw host.name value with the wrong mapping.
raw.title: Raw

meta.avoid_when: Avoid before confirming the canonical host mapping with the examiner or evidence. In DB-active mode WITHOUT a receipt recorder the correction is DENIED (fail closed).
meta.defer_loading_rationale: Rare, sensitive admin correction; Tool-Search `defer_loading` candidate.
meta.output_shape: FixHostMappingOut: status ('complete'), raw, new_canonical, docs_updated, host_identity_authority, host_identity_decision_id (DB receipt), audit_id, dict_path/dict_saved (legacy non-DB mode only), details{}. Mutating; returns no local filesystem paths in DB-active mode.
meta.response_shaping: Returns the correction receipt (decision/audit ids, docs_updated), not the reindexed documents.

usage_examples.description: Re-map a mis-attributed host across the case

meta.when_to_use: Correct a wrong host.id mapping in the active case: updates the host dictionary and reindexes docs where host.name equals raw. host.name is never changed. Use only when indexed host identity is known to be wrong.

tools.name: opensearch_fix_host_mapping

audit_id.description: Audit event id for the correction.
audit_id.title: Audit Id

dict_path.description: Legacy host dictionary path (legacy/non-DB-active mode only; omitted in DB-active mode where the dictionary is parser-compat).
dict_path.title: Dict Path

dict_saved.description: Whether the host dictionary was saved.
dict_saved.title: Dict Saved

host_identity_authority.description: Authority plane for host identity (postgres in DB-active mode).
host_identity_authority.title: Host Identity Authority

host_identity_decision_id.description: DB host-identity correction receipt id, when recorded.
host_identity_decision_id.title: Host Identity Decision Id

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.description: Check indexed filenames and services against the Windows baseline DB and stamp triage verdict fields. Use after ingest or after a baseline update. This remains behavior-compatible with the current synchronous path. Example: opensearch_enrich_triage().

meta.avoid_when: Do not treat an UNKNOWN baseline verdict as malicious by itself; verify interesting results against source records.
meta.output_shape: EnrichTriageOut: status ('complete'), documents_enriched, details{} (per-artifact enriched counts). Behavior-compatible synchronous path.
meta.response_shaping: Returns enriched counts only, never windows-triage credentials. Cannot approve findings, alter evidence, or decide reports.

usage_examples.description: Run Windows baseline enrichment on the active case
Enrich a named case

meta.when_to_use: Check indexed filenames/services against the Windows baseline DB and stamp triage.verdict on documents. Use after indexed Windows artifacts exist or after a baseline update. Requires enrichment:triage scope.

tools.name: opensearch_enrich_triage

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.title: Enrich: Windows Baseline Triage

tools.description: Extract unique IOCs from indexed docs, optionally look them up in OpenCTI, and stamp matching docs with threat_intel fields. Use dry_run=True to size the work, then dry_run=False to launch the existing async enrichment path. Example: opensearch_enrich_intel(dry_run=True).

dry_run.description: Extract and count IOCs without lookup.
dry_run.title: Dry Run

meta.avoid_when: Avoid when OpenCTI is unavailable or indicators are speculative. Use dry_run=True first to size the IOC corpus.
meta.output_shape: EnrichIntelOut: status (preview|started), case_id, ips, hashes, domains, total_iocs (preview counts), pid, run_id, log_file, note. ASYNC on write — returns a run reference; poll opensearch_ingest_status (artifact_name=='intel').
meta.response_shaping: Returns counts + a run reference, never IOC dumps or OpenCTI/OpenSearch credentials. Cannot approve findings, alter evidence, or decide reports.

usage_examples.description: Size the IOC corpus without lookup
Launch async enrichment for a case

meta.when_to_use: Extract unique IOCs from indexed docs and, with dry_run=False, look them up in OpenCTI and stamp matching docs with threat_intel fields. Use after indexed indicators exist and CTI context would help prioritize pivots. Requires enrichment:intel scope.

tools.name: opensearch_enrich_intel

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.title: Enrich: Threat Intel (OpenCTI)

tools.description: Return status for running or recent ingest and enrichment runs. Use to poll every roughly 30 seconds while a run is active and present the per-host artifact checklist. Default is active case; case_id='*' shows all cases. Example: opensearch_ingest_status().

case_id.description: Filter to this case; default active. '*' for all.
case_id.title: Case Id

meta.avoid_when: Operational status only — inspect indexed records (opensearch_search) for evidence, not this.
meta.defer_loading_rationale: Used only while an ingest/enrich run is in flight; Tool-Search `defer_loading` candidate.
meta.output_shape: IngestStatusOut: ingests[] of IngestRun{case_id, status, pid, elapsed, total_indexed, bulk_failed, hosts_complete/total, artifacts_complete/total, log_file, checklist[], message, halt_reason, errors[], next_steps[], warnings[], details{}}, message. case_id='*' shows all cases; default is the active case.
meta.response_shaping: Returns a structured progress summary plus a log_file reference, not the raw run log.

usage_examples.description: Poll the active case's runs
Show runs across all cases

meta.when_to_use: Poll running or recent ingest AND enrichment runs (~every 30s while active) and present the per-host artifact checklist. Also the status channel for async enrichment (poll with artifact_name 'intel'/'triage').

tools.name: opensearch_ingest_status

message.description: Summary message when no runs are present.
message.title: Message

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.title: Ingest/Enrichment Progress

tools.description: Preview (dry_run=True, default) or run evidence ingest into OpenSearch. Use dry_run=True first, review the plan, then dry_run=False with force=True only for intentional re-ingest. Execution remains behavior-compatible with the current async subprocess path. Example: opensearch_ingest(path='evidence/rocba-cdrive.e01', format='auto', dry_run=True).

all_logs.description: Parse all evtx, not only forensic logs.
all_logs.title: All Logs

force.description: Allow intentional re-ingest when the case already has docs.
force.title: Force

format.description: auto=containers/artifact dirs; otherwise choose a specific evidence format.
format.title: IngestFormat

hostname.description: Source hostname. Required for json/accesslog/memory and most delimited. 'auto' detects from filenames for flat delimited dirs.
hostname.title: Hostname

password.description: Archive/container password. SECRET - redacted from audit/logs/results.
password.title: Password

path.description: Evidence path under active case; bare names resolve to evidence/.
path.title: Path

recursive.description: Delimited dirs: treat immediate subdirs as hostnames.
recursive.title: Recursive

source_timezone.description: Evidence system local timezone, e.g. Eastern Standard Time.
source_timezone.title: Source Timezone

meta.avoid_when: Do NOT set dry_run=False until the target evidence and plan are clear. Use force=True only for an intentional re-ingest when the case already has docs.
meta.output_shape: IngestOut: status (preview|started|containers_detected|multi_started|already_indexed|failed), case_id, plan{}, container{}, already_indexed{}, suggested_hostname, warning, pid, run_id, log_file, note, details{}. Mutating; poll progress via opensearch_ingest_status.
meta.response_shaping: Returns a plan/run reference (run_id, log_file) rather than streaming ingest output — poll opensearch_ingest_status for progress. The 'password' arg is a SECRET, redacted from audit/logs/results.

usage_examples.description: Preview an auto-detected container (default dry run)
Ingest a specific delimited artifact set for one host
Deep memory analysis, write

meta.when_to_use: Discover and ingest forensic artifacts into OpenSearch after examiner approval. dry_run=True (default) previews the plan; set dry_run=False to write. Supports container/artifact-dir auto-detect, format override, include/exclude artifact filters, memory tiers/plugins, and VSS.

tools.name: opensearch_ingest

pid.description: Background process id for started runs.
pid.title: Pid

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.title: Ingest Evidence into OpenSearch

tools.description: Enumerate distinct values of a field with counts before writing targeted queries. Use for value discovery such as usernames or process names; prefer opensearch_aggregate when ranking matters. Example: opensearch_field_values(field='winlog.provider_name').

case_id.description: Case id. If empty, resolves to the active portal case (SIFT_CASE_DIR). Yields 'case-{id}-*'.
case_id.title: Case Id

field.description: Field to enumerate. CSV/text fields need '.keyword'.
field.title: Field

index.description: Index pattern; every segment MUST start with 'case-'. Overrides case_id when set. Leave empty to derive from case_id/active case.
index.title: Index

query.description: query_string filter to narrow the value set.
query.title: Query

meta.avoid_when: Use opensearch_aggregate instead when ranking/top-N matters; that path is the same terms aggregation framed for frequency.
meta.defer_loading_rationale: Overlaps opensearch_aggregate; lower-frequency discovery helper and a Tool-Search `defer_loading` candidate.
meta.output_shape: FieldValuesOut: field, values[] of {value, count}, truncated (true ⇒ more distinct values exist than returned).
meta.response_shaping: Returns the value inventory only. CSV/text fields need '.keyword'; raise limit (hard cap 500) to widen the inventory.

usage_examples.description: Distinct event-log providers present
Distinct users, filtered to a host

meta.when_to_use: Enumerate the distinct values of a field (with counts) before writing a narrower query — e.g. discover the usernames or process names present.

tools.name: opensearch_field_values

truncated.description: True when more distinct values exist than returned.
truncated.title: Truncated

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.title: Field Value Discovery

tools.description: Build a date histogram of event counts to find activity bursts before drilling in. Use to locate spikes, then scope opensearch_search with time_from/time_to. Buckets are warned at the configured ceiling and never silently truncated. Example: opensearch_timeline(query='event.code:4688', interval='1h').

case_id.description: Case id. If empty, resolves to the active portal case (SIFT_CASE_DIR). Yields 'case-{id}-*'.
case_id.title: Case Id

index.description: Index pattern; every segment MUST start with 'case-'. Overrides case_id when set. Leave empty to derive from case_id/active case.
index.title: Index

meta.avoid_when: Avoid for value distributions (use opensearch_aggregate) or for the events themselves (use opensearch_search).
meta.output_shape: TimelineOut: total_docs, interval, buckets[] of {time (ISO-8601), count} (sparse — empty buckets omitted), advisories[]. Buckets are warned at the configured ceiling and never silently truncated.
meta.response_shaping: Returns bucket counts only. Narrow with time_from/time_to or widen the interval (Ns/Nm/Nh/Nd) when an advisory flags a very large histogram.

usage_examples.description: Hourly histogram of process creations
15-minute resolution over a known window

meta.when_to_use: Find activity bursts: a date histogram of event counts over time. Locate spike windows, then scope opensearch_search with time_from/time_to.

tools.name: opensearch_timeline

count.description: Document count in this time bucket.
count.title: Count

advisories.description: Optional narrowing advisory for very large histograms.
advisories.title: Advisories

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.title: Event Timeline (Histogram)

tools.description: Fetch one complete document by _id with every field and no truncation. Use after opensearch_search when a compact hit needs inspection. The index must be exact, not a wildcard. Example: opensearch_get_event(event_id='abc123', index='case-rocba-drive-20260526-1417-evtx-srl-forge').

index.description: Exact case-* index name from the search hit; patterns are rejected.
index.title: Index

meta.avoid_when: Avoid for many documents (page opensearch_search instead) — this is a single-doc lookup.
meta.defer_loading_rationale: Used only to expand a known search hit; Tool-Search `defer_loading` candidate.
meta.output_shape: GetEventOut: id, index, fields (full, untruncated), truncated[] (empty), note. The index MUST be the exact case-* name from the hit, not a pattern.
meta.response_shaping: The deliberate counterpart to compact search: pay the full-document cost for exactly one record, identified from a prior search hit.

usage_examples.description: Expand a specific hit

meta.when_to_use: Fetch ONE complete document by _id with every field and no truncation — use after opensearch_search when a compact hit needs full inspection.

tools.name: opensearch_get_event

index.description: Concrete index the hit came from.
index.title: Index

truncated.description: Field names whose values were truncated to the size ceiling.
truncated.title: Truncated

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.title: Get Full Document

tools.description: Group by a field for top-N frequency analysis such as event codes, users, hosts, or process names. Use for distributions. Do not use when you want individual documents; use opensearch_search. Example: opensearch_aggregate(field='event.code').

case_id.description: Case id. If empty, resolves to the active portal case (SIFT_CASE_DIR). Yields 'case-{id}-*'.
case_id.title: Case Id

field.description: Field to group by. CSV/registry text fields need '.keyword' (e.g. 'Path.keyword'); evtx fields like event.code are already keyword.
field.title: Field

index.description: Index pattern; every segment MUST start with 'case-'. Overrides case_id when set. Leave empty to derive from case_id/active case.
index.title: Index

meta.avoid_when: Avoid when you want the individual documents (use opensearch_search) or a simple distinct value list without ranking (use opensearch_field_values).
meta.defer_loading_rationale: Frequent pivot tool; keep always-loaded alongside search/timeline.
meta.output_shape: AggregateOut: field, total_docs (matched before bucketing), buckets[] of {key, count}, truncated (true ⇒ more buckets than limit exist).
meta.response_shaping: Returns only the requested buckets, never raw docs — the canonical summary-over-bytes shape. CSV/registry text fields need '.keyword'; evtx fields like event.code are already keyword.

usage_examples.description: Top event codes in the active case
Top file paths (text field needs .keyword), filtered

meta.when_to_use: Top-N frequency analysis: group by a field (event codes, users, hosts, process names, IPs) with an optional query_string pre-filter.

tools.name: opensearch_aggregate

buckets.description: Top-N buckets for the requested field.
buckets.title: Buckets

truncated.description: True when bucket count hit the limit.
truncated.title: Truncated

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.title: Aggregate Field (Top-N)

tools.description: DEPRECATED tool form; prefer resource opensearch://cluster/status when available. Shows cluster health and per-case-index document counts. Use to confirm the cluster is reachable and see which cases have data; use opensearch_case_summary for one case's artifact and coverage breakdown.

meta.avoid_when: DEPRECATED tool form — prefer the resource opensearch://cluster/status. For one case's artifact/coverage breakdown use opensearch_case_summary.
meta.defer_loading_rationale: Deprecated tool form mirrored by a resource; Tool-Search `defer_loading` candidate pending removal at/after D27b.
meta.output_shape: StatusOut: cluster_status (single-node yellow may be annotated normal), indices[] of {index, docs, size, status}, total_indices. Health/status only — not case evidence.
meta.response_shaping: Compact cluster + index roll-up; also exposed as a read-only resource to keep it out of the tool budget.

usage_examples.description: Cluster + per-case index health

meta.when_to_use: Backend health/readiness check: cluster reachability plus per-case-index document counts. Confirms which cases have data.

tools.name: opensearch_status

status.description: Index status reported by cat indices.
status.title: Status

cluster_status.description: Cluster health status; single-node yellow may be annotated normal.
cluster_status.title: Cluster Status

indices.description: All case-* indices, sorted by name.
indices.title: Indices

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.title: Cluster & Index Status

tools.description: Survey a forensic container without mounting it: integrity, size, partitions, and available inspection details. Use before opensearch_ingest; follow with opensearch_ingest(dry_run=True) for the full plan. Example: opensearch_inspect_container(path='evidence/rocba-cdrive.e01').

path.description: Container path under the active case; bare names resolve to evidence/.
path.title: Path

meta.avoid_when: Avoid after ingest planning is done; follow this with opensearch_ingest(dry_run=True) for the full plan.
meta.defer_loading_rationale: SURVEY-phase tool used once per evidence item; Tool-Search `defer_loading` candidate.
meta.output_shape: InspectContainerOut: path, resolved_path, container_type (e01|raw|file|unknown), tool_available, size_bytes, size_human, hashes{}, partitions[], acquiry_info{}, raw_info (truncated).
meta.response_shaping: raw_info is truncated fdisk/img_stat text — a preview, not the full tool dump. Read-only; never modifies evidence.

usage_examples.description: Preview an E01 before ingest

meta.when_to_use: Survey a forensic container (E01/raw/zip) WITHOUT mounting it: integrity, size, partitions, acquisition metadata. Run during evidence survey before deciding what to ingest.

tools.name: opensearch_inspect_container

size_bytes.description: Container size in bytes if known.
size_bytes.title: Size Bytes

tool_available.description: False when no inspection tool is available on the SIFT VM.
tool_available.title: Tool Available

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.title: Inspect Forensic Container

tools.description: Complete coverage overview for a case. Call this first in every indexed session to see hosts, artifact families, doc counts, enrichment state, and coverage_state.gaps with exact commands to fill missing coverage. Example: opensearch_case_summary(case_id='rocba-drive-20260526-1417').

case_id.description: Case id; empty resolves to active case.
case_id.title: Case Id

include_fields.description: Include per-artifact field-type mappings; large output needed to decide '.keyword' suffixes.
include_fields.title: Include Fields

meta.avoid_when: Skip only when you already hold a current summary this session; for a single artifact's frequency use opensearch_aggregate instead.
meta.defer_loading_rationale: High-frequency entry point; keep always-loaded so the agent can orient at session start without a Tool-Search round trip.
meta.output_shape: CaseSummaryOut: case_id, hosts[], artifacts{family: {docs, hosts[], indices[]}}, total_docs, time_range{earliest, latest}, enrichment{...}, coverage_state{disk_artifacts, memory, enrichment, gaps[]}, investigation_hints[], warnings[]. Set include_fields=True only when you need per-artifact field/type maps to decide '.keyword' suffixes (large).
meta.response_shaping: include_fields defaults False to keep the summary compact; the field map is the bulk of the payload — request it only when picking aggregation fields.

usage_examples.description: Coverage map for the active case
Coverage map for a named case
Include field/type maps to plan aggregations

meta.when_to_use: Call FIRST in every indexed session. Returns hosts, artifact families, per-family doc counts, time range, enrichment state, and coverage_state.gaps with the exact ingest/enrich command to fill each gap — the map you plan the whole investigation from.

tools.name: opensearch_case_summary

docs.description: Document count for the artifact family.
docs.title: Docs

command.description: Exact ingest/enrich call to fill the gap.
command.title: Command

next_mcp_step.description: Next MCP step after filling the gap.
next_mcp_step.title: Next Mcp Step

when_to_run.description: When this gap should be filled.
when_to_run.title: When To Run

filesystem_meta_path.description: Most recent filesystem metadata sidecar path, relative to case dir.
filesystem_meta_path.title: Filesystem Meta Path

artifacts.description: Artifact coverage keyed by artifact family.
artifacts.title: Artifacts

fields_per_type.description: Optional capped field mappings per artifact type.
fields_per_type.title: Fields Per Type

investigation_hints.description: Compact investigation hints for indexed artifacts.
investigation_hints.title: Investigation Hints

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.title: Case Coverage Summary

tools.description: DEPRECATED tool form; prefer resource opensearch://cluster/shards when available. Reports shard usage and capacity headroom. Use before large ingests because a full disk image can add many shards.

meta.avoid_when: DEPRECATED tool form — prefer the resource opensearch://cluster/shards. Administrative signal, not investigation evidence.
meta.defer_loading_rationale: Deprecated, operations-only; mirrored by a resource. Tool-Search `defer_loading` candidate pending removal at/after D27b.
meta.output_shape: ShardStatusOut: current_shards, max_shards_per_node, data_nodes, max_total, headroom_pct, status (ok>=10% | warning>=2% | critical<2%), top_indices_by_shard_count[].
meta.response_shaping: Capacity roll-up with only the top shard-heavy indices; also exposed as a read-only resource.

usage_examples.description: Check shard headroom before a large image ingest

meta.when_to_use: Operational readiness before a large ingest (a full disk image can add many shards) or when indexing fails: shard usage and capacity headroom.

tools.name: opensearch_shard_status

max_shards_per_node.description: Configured max shards per data node.
max_shards_per_node.title: Max Shards Per Node

max_total.description: Maximum total shards across data nodes.
max_total.title: Max Total

top_indices_by_shard_count.description: Top visible indices by shard count.
top_indices_by_shard_count.title: Top Indices By Shard Count

remediation.description: Concrete next step the caller can take.
remediation.title: Remediation

retryable.description: True if retrying the same call may succeed (e.g. transient upstream loss).
retryable.title: Retryable

tools.title: Shard Capacity
